thread.section_title: North Myrtle Beach, SC Local News and More | NewsBreak

author: NewsBreak Original
text:
By Gabby Jonas ,
1 hours ago
CONWAY, S.C. — New Horry County Police Chief Kris Leonhardt, in his 23rd year with the department, said he is excited and humbled to be able to tackle the new leadership role.
Leonhardt, who was sworn in on Oct. 1, said everything has gone according to plan since he took on the role following the retirement of Joseph Hill, who was the chief for eight years.
“My vision is to continue to grow,” he said. “Look at those units that we continue to enhance, to help the quality of life for our citizens in Horry County. I’m very transparent. I love being transparent, getting out into the community. I want our teams to be a part of our community, serve our community, get to know them.”
Newly added repeat-offender and gang-unit groups will help keep the county safe, Leonhardt said.
After a recent incident of a 13-year-old boy being allegedly physically assaulted and yelled at by the suspect using racial slurs at a basketball court on Clearwater Drive near Surfside Beach, Leonhardt said it’s those kinds of cases that the department takes seriously and will do everything they can to help.
“We’ve definitely been investigating that case, and with any case, we’re going to investigate it to the fullest extent,” he said. “Our detectives and I get updates on a daily basis and are digging into the case, trying to identify folks that were involved. We’ll do that. That’s what we’re here for is to bring justice to the cases.”
With his agency being the largest in the area, Leonhardt said the department will continue to be competitive with other agencies when recruiting future officers. However, in the end, he said they are all a team working together to try to help the community.
Every month, Leonhardt said local chiefs, sheriffs and some federal partners meet to discuss topics that are important to all of the agencies in the community and to decide how they can better serve each other.
“We have a great agency,” he said. “We have great partners, and I just feel like we’re going in the right direction and we’ll continue to do that.”
Leonhardt said his passion for law enforcement and the fact that he has worked in almost every division in the department, made his desire to follow in Hill’s footsteps an obvious decision.
“The passion that I have for our agency, I just always looked for those challenges to continue to help myself grow,” he said. “As that continued throughout my career, it seemed like a goal for me that I wanted to achieve to continue to help the department grow,” he said.
* * *
Gabby Jonas joined the News13 team as a multimedia journalist in April 2024. She is from Columbus, Ohio, and graduated from Kent State University in May 2023. Follow Gabby on X, formerly Twitter , Facebook or Instagram , and read more of her work here .
